label: layoff_grief_anger
entry: Got the call today. Position eliminated. 8 years and they did it over Zoom in 12 minutes. I keep replaying the HR person's face — she looked like she'd done this 40 times today and mine was just another slot. I don't even know what I feel yet. Angry? Relieved? Terrified? All of it at once, maybe.

label: very_short_entry
entry: Tired. Don't want to write today.

label: crisis_language
entry: I honestly don't know if I can keep doing this. Everything feels pointless. I wake up and the first thought is 'what's the point.' I'm not going to do anything stupid but I just want to disappear for a while. Maybe a long while.

label: vague_avoidant
entry: Things are fine. Work is work. Had dinner with some friends. Nothing really to report. Just checking in I guess.

label: high_energy_breakthrough
entry: I finally said no. My mother asked me to cancel my weekend plans to come help her rearrange furniture — something she's perfectly capable of doing herself — and I said no. Not rudely. Just 'I have plans this weekend, but I'm free next Thursday if you'd like help then.' She went quiet. I felt guilty for about 20 minutes and then I felt... free? Like a door opened. It's such a small thing but it doesn't feel small.

label: new_manager_anxiety
entry: New boss starts Monday. Everyone's acting normal but I can feel the tension. She's coming from Amazon and apparently she's 'data-driven' which in my experience means micromanagement dressed up in dashboards. I've been in this role 3 years and suddenly I feel like I need to prove myself all over again. Already started a doc of all my wins from the last quarter. Hate that I'm doing that.

label: international_move_isolation
entry: It's been 4 months in Amsterdam and I still eat dinner alone most nights. I have colleagues but they all have their lives, their established friend groups, their weekend plans that don't include the new person. I miss walking into my kitchen back home and my roommate being there. I miss being known. Here I'm just the person who moved from Canada.

label: relationship_conflict
entry: Umut and I had the same fight again. Different words, same fight. He says I shut down when things get hard. I say he escalates when I need space. We're both right. We're both doing the thing the other person can't handle. I love him but sometimes I wonder if love is enough when your conflict styles are this incompatible.

label: perfectionism_paralysis
entry: I've rewritten the opening paragraph of my business plan 14 times. Fourteen. I know this is perfectionism. I know the first version was probably fine. But something in me won't let it go until it's 'right' and I can't even define what right looks like. Meanwhile the actual work isn't getting done because I'm polishing the introduction nobody will read.

label: identity_crisis_career
entry: Someone asked me at a party what I do and I froze. I used to say 'product manager at Stripe' without thinking. Now I'm... what? 'Exploring options'? 'Between things'? 'Finding myself at 37'? I didn't realise how much of my identity was wrapped up in that job title until it was gone. Who am I without the company logo?

label: inner_critic_attack
entry: The voice in my head has been relentless today. You're not good enough. You'll fail. Everyone else has it figured out. You're behind. It started when I saw a former colleague's LinkedIn post about her promotion and it's been running on a loop since 7am. I know it's my inner critic. I know it's not true. But knowing doesn't make it stop.

label: somatic_awareness
entry: Noticed my shoulders were at my ears during the entire team meeting. Like literally touching my ears. When I consciously dropped them, I felt this rush of emotion — not sadness exactly, more like the tension was holding something in place and when it released, everything underneath moved. My body is carrying something my mind hasn't processed yet.

label: cultural_adjustment
entry: Had a meeting with my Dutch colleagues and they told me my proposal was 'not very good' in front of everyone. Just like that. In Canada, that would be career suicide. Here it's Tuesday. I know directness is the culture. I even admire it intellectually. But my nervous system doesn't know the difference between Dutch directness and personal attack. I went home and stress-ate an entire bag of stroopwafels.

label: gratitude_and_growth
entry: Looking back at where I was 3 months ago vs now — I'm actually proud of myself. I've journalled every day for 6 weeks. I've had two hard conversations I would have avoided before. I set a boundary with my sister that I've been avoiding for years. It's not perfect and I'm not 'fixed' but the direction is right. Movement matters more than arrival.

label: people_pleasing_pattern
entry: Said yes to three things this week I didn't want to do. A networking event I dread, a project that's not mine to own, and covering for a colleague who always has emergencies. I know the pattern. I know I'm doing it. I even catch myself mid-yes sometimes and still can't stop. The approval feels like oxygen. The resentment comes later.

label: family_of_origin
entry: Called my dad for his birthday. He spent 20 minutes talking about my brother's new house. Not one question about my life. I'm 35 and I still wait for him to ask. I know he loves me but his love looks like ignoring me unless I'm in crisis. I learned early that being low-maintenance meant being invisible.

label: control_and_uncertainty
entry: I've made 4 different spreadsheets for the move. Timeline, budget, contacts, logistics. I have backup plans for my backup plans. My partner pointed out that I'm trying to control something that's fundamentally uncontrollable and I snapped at her. She's right. I know she's right. The planning isn't about the move anymore — it's about managing my terror of the unknown.

label: vulnerability_avoidance
entry: My therapist asked me to describe what I'm feeling and I immediately went analytical. 'Well, the situation involves...' She stopped me. 'Not what you're thinking. What you're feeling.' I genuinely didn't know. Like the signal from my body to my brain has a bad connection. I could describe the situation in perfect detail but couldn't name a single emotion.

label: purpose_searching
entry: I keep asking myself what I actually want to do with my career and the honest answer is I don't know. I know what I'm good at. I know what pays well. I know what impresses people. But when I strip all that away and ask what actually lights me up... silence. Like I've been so busy building what I should want that I forgot to check in with what I actually want.

label: anger_and_boundary
entry: I'm furious. My cofounder just told our biggest client we'd deliver the feature by Friday WITHOUT ASKING ME. I'm the one who has to build it. I'm the one who'll be up until 2am. And when I pushed back he said 'we need to be flexible.' No, YOU need to be flexible. I need to be respected. I didn't say that. I smiled and said we'd figure it out. I hate myself for that.

label: grief_unexpected
entry: Cleaning out the apartment and I found the mug from our first trip to Portugal. It's just a mug. It's ugly, honestly. But I sat on the kitchen floor holding it for 20 minutes. The grief isn't about him anymore, I think. It's about the version of me that existed in that relationship — the one who believed certain things about her future that aren't true anymore.

label: enneagram_type_recognition
entry: Read about Enneagram Type 1 and felt personally attacked. The constant inner judge. The 'right way' to do everything. The resentment when others don't meet my standards but I'd never admit I have standards because that would make me rigid and I definitely don't want to be rigid even though I absolutely am.

label: multi_theme_complex
entry: Started the new job, had a panic attack in the bathroom on day 2. Texted my ex for comfort which I know is unhealthy. Then went home and rage-cleaned my apartment until midnight. Three coping mechanisms in one day, all of them avoidance. The new manager seems fine but I keep waiting for the other shoe to drop. My body remembers what my mind is trying to forget about the last workplace.

label: minimalist_check_in
entry: ok day

label: intellectual_deflection
entry: I've been reading about attachment theory and I find it fascinating from an academic perspective. The research on anxious-avoidant pairings is particularly compelling. I think understanding these dynamics is important for anyone in a relationship. It's useful to know about these patterns. I've been recommending books on the topic to friends.

label: transition_excitement_fear
entry: The visa came through. We're actually moving to Berlin. I screamed when I saw the email and then immediately opened a spreadsheet. The excitement and the terror are the same feeling, I think? Like my body can't tell the difference between 'this is everything I wanted' and 'what have I done.' Both are true at the same time.

label: burnout_recognition
entry: I used to love this work. I used to get excited on Sunday nights. Now I wake up Monday with a knot in my stomach that doesn't unwind until Friday at 6pm. It's not the work itself — the work is interesting. It's the relentless pace, the always-on culture, the Slack messages at 10pm that say 'quick question' but are never quick. I'm burning out and I can feel it happening in slow motion.

label: positive_relationship_moment
entry: Umut made dinner and it was terrible. Absolutely terrible. Burnt rice, oversalted everything. And he was so proud of it. I ate the whole thing. Not because I'm a people pleaser (well, partly) but because watching someone you love try something new and care about it is genuinely beautiful. I told him it was good. He knew I was lying. We laughed about it. That's the relationship I want — the one where we can be bad at things together.

label: authority_challenge
entry: My skip-level asked for my 'honest feedback' about the reorg in our 1:1. Right. Honest feedback. To the VP who designed the reorg. I gave her the sanitised version and she could tell. She pushed. I gave her 40% of the truth. She seemed to appreciate it. But here's the thing — in my family, telling people with power the truth got you punished. I know she's not my mother. My nervous system doesn't.

label: self_compassion_emerging
entry: Something shifted today. I made a mistake in a presentation — said the wrong number, got corrected in front of 20 people — and instead of the usual spiral, I just... let it be. Thought 'that was embarrassing' the way you'd think 'that was a red car.' Noticed it, didn't attach to it, moved on. That has NEVER happened before. Usually I'd replay it for days. Maybe all this work is actually doing something.
